title: Upload Admission, Untrusted Files, and Quarantine · Go Semantics Case: Media Type Sniff Policy
summary: An upload handler trusts client filenames and content types, accepts unlimited bodies, publishes bytes before scanning, and leaves partial state when the caller disconnects. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the media type sniff policy increment in bounded upload-admission and quarantine workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Combine bounded signature inspection, declared media type, extension, parser or scanner evidence, and allow-list policy without executing content.
Treat filenames as display metadata, normalize safely, reject controls and path confusion, and generate storage keys independently.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Media Type Sniff Policy · Theory Media Type Sniff Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 2796 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound upload bytes and concurrency before quarantined publication state; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
